name: Spike Lee
abstract: Shelton Jackson "Spike" Lee (born March 20, 1957) is an American filmmaker and actor. Cited by various publications as one of the most important filmmakers of the late 20th century, his work explores issues including race relations, issues within the black community, the role of media in contemporary life, urban crime and poverty.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Movie Music of Spike Lee and Terence Blanchard
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

concerts.label: NAN 30th Anniversary and Reverend Al Sharpton's Belated Birthday Celebration
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: JJ Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: We were just talking this morning about Terence Blanchard and the fact that Spike Lee is using him a lot.
about_quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Branford Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: So, Spike asked me to be in a movie. He told me what the movie was about. It was about HBCU’s [historically black colleges and universities]. I said, “Well, I went to an HBCU. I’ll do this shit for free. You gonna pay me to act like a crazy man like I did in college? OK, great.” So, we were in Atlan
about_quotes.doc_title: Branford Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Kent Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: I don't know if you saw the Spike Lee film or saw portions of it, but it is really a devastating experience just to see entire neighborhoods wiped off the face of the earth you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Kent Jordan interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 22, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Clark Terry
about_quotes.evidence: A lot of little Nicholas Paytons or the little guy that wrote what’s his name’s book — what’s that guy that wrote that, the little black director. >> [MW] Spike Lee?
about_quotes.doc_title: Clark Terry interviewed by Joe Williams and Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, May 19, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Slide Hampton
about_quotes.evidence: Around the corner from me lived Bill Lee, the composer. He used to come by there a lot. ... That would be Spike Lee’s father? Yes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Slide Hampton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Hubert Laws
about_quotes.evidence: I remember him vividly being one of the bass players that came in there... I remember sitting in on that stuff.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hubert Laws — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
